'''Gareth Stephens''' (born 15 April 1974) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for Great Britain (Under-21s), and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] where he signed a record schoolboy contract and made his first team debut at the age of 17, [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull FC]], [[Sheffield Eagles]] and [[York Wasps]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Stephens was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, and he is the son of [[Gary Stephens]],<ref>{{cite web|last1=Robinson|first1=John|title=RL bid to keep Lydon at 'home'|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/RL+bid+to+keep+Lydon+at+%60home%27.-a061145826|website=The People|accessdate=26 January 2015|location=London|date=9 February 1997}}</ref> former [[rugby league]] international footballer of the 1960s, 1970s and 1980s. Gareth Stephens is also the cousin of Dean Hanson,<ref>{{Cite web|title=Dean Hanson - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/dean-hanson/summary.html|access-date=2020-08-11|website=rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> the rugby league {{rlp|pr|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}} of the 1980s and 1990s for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Illawarra Steelers]].

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Stephens started his career at [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], making his début in September 1991.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Whalley|first1=John|title=Rugby League: Saints keep unbeaten run|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=23 September 1991|location=London|page=39}}</ref> He was signed by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] for £65000 in July 1994.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Fitzpatrick|first1=Paul|title=Castleford invest in future by signing Stephens and Goddard|work=The Guardian|date=12 July 1994|location=London|page=15}}</ref> He went on to play for [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]] and [[York Wasps]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Extended Shay Stay For Gareth|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/extended-shay-stay-for-gareth-1-1991810|website=Halifax Courier|accessdate=26 January 2015|date=8 March 2000}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Backs to the future|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/7953172.print|website=Telegraph & Argus|publisher=Newsquest Media Group|accessdate=26 January 2015|date=26 August 2000}}</ref>

===International honours===
Stephens won caps at Great Britain academy and U21 level along with four caps for Wales between 1995 and 1998, including at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>
